articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Appoints Kelly Garcia As Chief Technology Officer
articles.summary: Ulta Beauty has appointed Kelly Garcia as Chief Technology Officer (CTO), strengthening its executive leadership team to support the company’s technology and digital growth strategy. Effective August 31, 2026, Garcia will join Ulta Beauty’s executive team after serving on the company’s Board of Directors since 2022, from which he will resign upon assuming the role. He most recently served as Executive Vice President and CTO at Domino’s Pizza, where he led the company’s global technology strategy for more than a decade. Garcia brings over 25 years of experience spanning e-commerce, customer loyalty, digital innovation, artificial intelligence and cybersecurity.

articles.headline: Estee Lauder Cos. Decides Not To Sell Dr.Jart, Too Faced & Smashbox
articles.author: Rachel Klemovitch
articles.summary: According to recent reports, Estee Lauder Companies (ELC) has decided against selling Dr.Jart, Too Faced, and Smashbox. Rather than selling the brands, ELC has explored different strategic options and chose to restructure the brands’ operating models. These restructurings will help increase growth and profitability. Too Faced will relocate its headquarters from Los Angeles to New York and join the company’s makeup cluster alongside MAC and Bobbi Brown with a significantly streamlined team. While no president has been named to oversee Too Faced, Lisa Sequino is currently the president of the makeup cluster that Too Faced joins.

articles.headline: L’Oréal, MAC, And Clinique Fans Warned Of New Law BANNING Makeup Products Over Hidden Ingredient
articles.author: Grace Harrington
articles.summary: TOP makeup brands are being forced to abide by a new statewide law banning so-called forever chemicals from beauty products. The law will affect L’Oréal, MAC, Clinique and all other makeup manufacturers. A new Illinois law is banning the intentional addition of polyfluoroalkyl substances, or PFAS, in cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, and intimate apparel. PFAS are synthetic chemicals designed to make products resistant to heat, water, oil, or stains.

articles.headline: After The Estée Lauder-Puig Collapse, Which Beauty Mergers Would Actually Work?
articles.author: Rachel Brown
articles.summary: The proposed marriage between Estée Lauder and Puig collapsed in May before it was ever consummated. Since the engagement was called off, Lauder’s shares have climbed roughly 4%. Puig’s shares, however, are down more than 4%. The collapse underscored a blunt reality: the merger that made sense on paper (Lauder would gain enviable fragrance assets while Puig would achieve greater scale) quickly unraveled when confronted with the complexities of integration. Without rehashing all the sordid details of the blame game, from Charlotte Tilbury’s contract negotiations to disagreements over price, the deal ultimately appeared too messy for both the companies and investors, who wanted portfolio clarity and discipline but instead saw a clash of family-controlled dynasties and the prospect of prolonged post-acquisition pain.

articles.headline: L’Oréal Becomes The Exclusive Beauty Partner Of The Plenitude Arena
articles.summary: The L’Oréal Group and the Plenitude Arena announce the signing of an exclusive, renewable two-year partnership. This partnership places the world leader in beauty and its 40 international brands at the heart of the world’s greatest cultural and sporting events, hosted by Europe’s largest indoor venue (up to 45,000 seats), located in Nanterre, on the outskirts of Paris. Recently renamed Plenitude Arena as of July 1, 2026, the venue has just begun a new chapter in its history, driven by renewed ambitions and the arrival of leading partners to support its world-renowned events.

articles.headline: Chanel Dares To Rework Its Iconic Watch: Will The New J12 Make Us Forget The Original?
articles.author: Mila Lazri
articles.summary: The Chanel J12 Calibre 12.1 38 mm opens a new chapter for a watch that has become one of the Parisian house’s most recognizable models. More than twenty-five years after its launch, Chanel has chosen to update its icon without disrupting the silhouette that built its success. This new interpretation joins a collection created in 2000 under the artistic direction of Jacques Helleu. Inspired by racing cars and J-Class sailing yachts, the J12 helped establish ceramic in the world of high-end sports watches. Following the glossy black of the original version and the white model introduced in 2003, Chanel is now exploring a more understated expression of its flagship watch.Produits de luxe

articles.headline: Gucci Claimed A Monte Carlo Beach Club Through October: 4 More Luxury Pop-Ups Still Open
articles.author: Edward Mills
articles.summary: This is mid-July, which means you are squarely inside the window. Six luxury fashion houses have taken over some of the most storied beach clubs and coastal hotels in Europe — from Monaco to Portofino to Ibiza — and every single one of them is still open. The first to close, Burberry on the French Riviera and Chloé in Formentera, runs through September 30. Gucci's Monaco takeover and Jacquemus's Bodrum debut hold until the end of October. What used to be a niche marketing move — a branded sunbed here, a logo parasol there — has grown into a genuine travel phenomenon, with fashion houses now designing full seasonal programs around specific coastal addresses, right down to the cocktails, the bicycles, and the restaurant menus. Here is where they are, what each one looks like in practice, and the critical detail most coverage leaves out: which ones you can walk into without booking a room.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Welcomes Monpure London
articles.author: Rachel Klemovitch
articles.summary: Monpure London, the trichologist-backed British hair and scalp health brand, adds Ulta Beauty to a growing U.S. retail roster that already includes Nordstrom, Bloomingdale’s and Mybeautyspace.com. Scalp health has shifted from niche interest to mainstream priority in the U.S., with bond repair following close behind. Monpure’s position, built on clinical research and trichologist insight rather than trend, places it among a small number of brands with the credibility to lead that shift at retail.

articles.headline: Ingredient-Led Brands Are Dominating AI Beauty Citations
articles.author: Emily Jensen
articles.summary: The Ordinary shook up the beauty aisle when it launched in 2016 with affordable skin-care formulations built around a single active ingredient, like hyaluronic acid or caffeine. A decade later, that approach appears to be helping the Estée Lauder-owned skin-care brand in a new era of beauty consumption. According to data from 5W AI Communications, The Ordinary tops the list of beauty brands surfaced in AI citations. The brand’s products appeared in 7% of responses to beauty-related consumer prompts submitted to ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews. Skin-care brands largely dominated the top results, with CeraVe and La Roche-Posay also in the top five brands by AI citations.

articles.headline: Glamsquad Is Redefining The Beauty Experience
articles.summary: David Goldweitz, cofounder and chief executive officer of Glamsquad, and Giovanni Vaccaro, cofounder of Glamsquad, discuss with Fairchild Studio how the beauty services platform is shaping the future of beauty experiences with tech-driven personalization and brand partnerships.

articles.headline: Wellness Briefing: Is Hormone Tracking The Future Of Women’s Wearables? Plus News
articles.author: Lexy Lebsack
articles.summary: For the Wellness Briefing, Glossy interviewed Jenny Duan, the 22-year-old founder who has raised $11.6 million to launch a continuous hormone-tracking wearable named Clair. It’s part of the burgeoning hormone-tracking marketplace that could soon upend how women interact with wearables. Additionally, NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani announces a new “click to cancel” law that will soon impact gyms and subscription-based CPG. Plus, a new stat illustrates spa worker burnout, and CorePower Yoga and Gold’s Gym experience exec shakeups.

articles.headline: Influencer Marketing Insights: Traackr And Unilever Discuss Creator Collaborations
articles.summary: At the WWD Beauty CEO Summit, Pierre-Loïc Assayag, cofounder and chief executive officer of Traackr, and Casey DePalma McCartney, chief strategy and brand communications officer at Unilever U.S., sat down with Amanda Smith, CEO of Fairchild Media Group, to share how influencer marketing is evolving. This special episode of WWD Voices features their live discussion from the mainstage. During the panel, speakers noted the importance of establishing relationships with creators and social listening, which together enable brands to react to trending online conversations quickly and authentically.

articles.headline: From Chanel To Dior, The Greek Goddess Effect Endures
articles.author: Tonya Blazio-Licorish
articles.summary: Zendaya’s recent red carpet appearance for summer’s presumed next blockbuster film “The Odyssey” reminded us how Greek dress styles have consistently inspired designer collections through the years. From Chanel to Dior and everywhere in between, Grecian-style draping, flowing pleats and bare-shoulder looks in floor-length to mini silhouettes may echo the past, but continue to resonate as one of fashion’s most enduring sources of inspiration. Across fashion’s timelines, dress codes from ancient Greece — from the chiton to the peplos — have created statement-making moments on and off the runway. While early successes can easily be connected to French couturier Madame Grès, whose meticulously draped and pleated column evening dresses remain iconic, many designers have since added to the conversation.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Hit With Patent Suit Over Store Locator Features
articles.author: Riya Sharma
articles.summary: Ulta Salon, Cosmetics & Fragrance, which does business as Ulta Beauty, was accused of patent infringement over location-based wireless service methods. The beauty retailer used a store locator feature on its app and website that allegedly infringed on two patents — US Nos. 7,532,899 and 8,774,834 — owned by non-practicing entity Local Interest LLC that cover methods for locating sets of desired locations. The complaint was filed Wednesday in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas.

articles.headline: The Estée Lauder Companies Appoints Madeleine Boyd As Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications
articles.summary: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. (NYSE: EL) today announced the appointment of Madeleine Boyd as Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications, effective July 20, 2026. As part of the company’s continued efforts to strengthen how its brands better connect with consumers, Ms. Boyd will establish and lead a newly integrated Global Brand Communications team. In this role, she will ensure the company’s diverse portfolio is anchored by a cohesive enterprise communications strategy, while accelerating bold, consumer-first storytelling that drives earned media, cultural relevance, and brand desirability. She will also strengthen creator engagement, helping the company’s brands gain attention where culture is being shaped.

articles.headline: Chanel Says No More Shredding As Unsold Goods Shift To L’Atelier Des Matières
articles.author: Tianwei Zhang
articles.summary: After a court disclosure revealed thousands of products were shredded in Hong Kong every six months back in 2017, Chanel points to L’Atelier des Matières and new circular unit Nevold as proof that unsold goods are now recycled, not destroyed. Chanel is pushing back against figures recently disclosed in a Hong Kong courtroom, stressing they don’t reflect how the brand now handles unsold goods as Europe tightens the rules on fashion waste.

articles.headline: Chanel CFO Philippe Blondiaux To Retire At End Of 2026
articles.summary: The longtime finance executive will step down after 15 years with the luxury house, with a successor to be announced at a later date. Chanel global chief financial officer Philippe Blondiaux will retire at the end of 2026, concluding a 15-year tenure at the privately held luxury house. Blondiaux joined Chanel in 2011 following an international career spanning finance and business leadership roles at Nestlé and PwC. Over the following decade and a half, he became one of the company’s most visible senior executives, regularly representing Chanel in discussions of its financial performance, investment strategy and long-term approach to growth.

articles.headline: Destroy Or Resell: The Impossible Equation For Luxury Giants Facing Their Own Stocks
articles.author: Julie Boone
articles.summary: Long kept as one of the best-guarded secrets in the luxury industry, the destruction of unsold goods is now caught between ethical questioning and legislative constraints. Behind the scenes of haute couture, prestige has long been measured by what did not sell. To preserve this sense of exclusivity, destroying exceptional pieces rather than discounting them was a tacit but absolute rule. Today, faced with societal pressure and unprecedented regulations, the industry must learn to manage its end-of-cycle products without damaging its image. It is a paradox that has long plagued the fashion industry: artisans spend dozens of hours crafting exceptional bags and garments, only for these very objects to sometimes end up shredded or incinerated in the secrecy of high-security industrial zones.

articles.headline: Luxury Retailer’s Employee Criminal Trial Exposes Industry Practice
articles.author: Fernanda Tronco
articles.summary: One of the world’s most recognizable luxury fashion brands is at the center of a yearslong criminal trial after two former warehouse employees were accused of stealing hundreds of high-end products that had been designated for destruction. Beyond the alleged theft, testimony in the case has pulled back the curtain on a little-known practice in the luxury industry: destroying unsold merchandise to preserve exclusivity rather than allowing it to be sold at discounted prices.

articles.headline: Treatment Stacking To TPE: The Next Aesthetic Treatment Trends To Know
articles.author: Amy Francombe
articles.summary: The modern aesthetics industry, long plagued by fillers, lasers, Botox, and the nips and tucks of aging skin, is sitting on the precipice of its next era. As the age-old act of going under the knife retreats into the background, the next cohort of treatments is waiting in the wings. But this time, the focus goes even deeper than an injectable, with more consumers becoming hyperfixated on skin quality, collagen stimulation, inflammation, hormonal changes, metabolic health, or how to preserve their appearance over decades, not just years.

articles.headline: Regulations Have Been Developed For The Beauty Industry
articles.author: Tatiana Sichirliiscaia
articles.summary: The State Tax Service has developed guidelines for providing beauty services. The document is intended to help individuals formalize their business activities. Today, they can operate under the specialty “Hairdressing and Beauty Salon Services,” as defined in the Classification of Economic Activities, and also carry out the activity “Hairdressing and Cosmetic Services” under a business license. The document is available on the tax authority’s official website. It includes information on the types of economic activities in this field. It is emphasized, however, that this does not include the provision of medical services.

articles.headline: Paris Hilton Returns To NYX Professional Makeup As The Gloss Boss
articles.author: Lollie Hancock
articles.summary: NYX Professional Makeup has named socialite-turned-entrepreneur Paris Hilton as its new Gloss Boss. The appointment comes as part of the next chapter of the L’Oréal-owned make-up brand’s annual campaign, ‘If You NYX, You Know’ (IYNYK). Hilton, who was part of the first installment of IYNYK, which debuted last summer, is welcomed back amongst a roster of celebrity partners, including pop star and ‘Love Lies’ singer Normani, Love Island UK’s Toni Laites, Brazilian model and content creator Gabriela Moura and Middle Eastern beauty creator Yusur Al-khalidi.
